summary: ready-by rejects Hayward -1 / 0 / negative water-temp sentinels as real readings via the new pickWaterTempForReadyBy helper.
reason: Greptile P1 #3216365845. The original water-temp guard only caught currentTemp==0; Hayward's standard 'sensor not reading' sentinel is -1 (normal pre-season state and the default state on installs whose temp sensor only reads while the pump runs, i.e. capabilities.temp_needs_flow=true). With water_temp=-1 the old code computed delta=targetTemp+1 -> hoursNeeded~56.7 -> startAt~2 days in the past, then fired the 'behind schedule' branch, enabled the heater, and wrote a 'successful setpoint operation' row to command_log — all from bogus sensor data. Fix: extract pickWaterTempForReadyBy(tele, caps), reject any non-positive reading explicitly, and produce a capability-aware error that names the -1 sentinel and tells temp_needs_flow=true operators to start the filter pump first.
validated_outcome: TestPickWaterTempForReadyBy covers 6 scenarios (valid reading, -1 rejected, -1 with flow-needs hint, 0 rejected, no reading, nil telemetry). Live-verified against a real Hayward account with water_temp=-1 and pump idle: command exits non-zero with the actionable hint instead of silently firing the heater.
